units.sanskrit: ओं सहनावितिशान्तिः । ओं उशन्ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसंददौ । तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ॥ १ ॥
units.transliteration: oṃ sahanāvitiśāntiḥ | oṃ uśanha vai vājaśravasaḥ sarvavedasaṃdadau | tasya ha naciketā nāma putra āsa || 1 ||
units.translation: From desire, as story tells, the son of Vajasrava made a gift of all his wealth; he had a son, so the story goes, by name Nachiketas.

units.sanskrit: तँ ह कुमारँ सन्तं दक्षिणासु नीयमानासु श्रद्धाऽऽविवेश सोऽमन्यत ॥ २ ॥
units.transliteration: tam̐ ha kumāram̐ santaṃ dakṣiṇāsu nīyamānāsu śraddhā''viveśa so'manyata || 2 ||
units.translation: Him, though young, zeal possessed when rewards were being distributed; he thought:

units.sanskrit: पीतोदका जग्धतृणा दुग्धदोहा निरिन्द्रियाः । अनन्दा नाम ते लोकास्तान्स गच्छति ता ददत् ॥ ३ ॥
units.transliteration: pītodakā jagdhatṛṇā dugdhadohā nirindriyāḥ | anandā nāma te lokāstānsa gacchati tā dadat || 3 ||
units.translation: (These cows) have drunk water for the last time, eaten grass for the last time, have yielded all their milk and are devoid of vigour. Joyless verily are those worlds; them he attains who gives these.

units.sanskrit: स होवाच पितरं तत कस्मै मां दास्यसीति । द्वितीयं तृतीयं तँ होवाच मृत्यवे त्वा ददामीति ॥ ४ ॥
units.transliteration: sa hovāca pitaraṃ tata kasmai māṃ dāsyasīti | dvitīyaṃ tṛtīyaṃ tam̐ hovāca mṛtyave tvā dadāmīti || 4 ||
units.translation: He said unto his sire, 'father, to whom wilt thou give me'; he said this again and for the third time. To him, he said 'unto Death do I give thee.'

units.sanskrit: बहूनामेमि प्रथमो बहूनामेमि मध्यमः । किँ स्विद्यमस्य कर्तव्यं यन्मयाद्य करिष्यति ॥ ५ ॥
units.transliteration: bahūnāmemi prathamo bahūnāmemi madhyamaḥ | kim̐ svidyamasya kartavyaṃ yanmayādya kariṣyati || 5 ||
units.translation: Of many I go the first; of many I go midmost; what is there for Death to do which he can now do by me?

units.sanskrit: अनुपश्य यथा पूर्वे प्रतिपश्य तथापरे । सस्यमिव मर्त्यः पच्यते सस्यमिवाजायते पुनः ॥ ६ ॥
units.transliteration: anupaśya yathā pūrve pratipaśya tathāpare | sasyamiva martyaḥ pacyate sasyamivājāyate punaḥ || 6 ||
units.translation: Call to mind how our ancestors behaved and mark also how others now behave; like corn, decays the mortal and like corn is born again.
